query: registry of available embedding backends with dimensions and model notes
handler that accepts an embedder name and queues a reindex job
CocoIndex configuration that chooses the default local code embedding model
shell validator for spec folders with strict mode and recursive phase support
Vitest coverage for code graph readiness snapshots and stale state
construct an Ollama-backed embedder and apply query/document prefixes before embedding
single scoring stage where recency, graph, feedback, and rescue boosts are combined
readiness probe that checks whether the semantic code index is fresh or absent
declarative list of vetted sentence transformer candidates for local code search
context save command that reads structured JSON and refreshes graph metadata
Apple Silicon device fallback that prefers Metal when CUDA is unavailable
paraphrase recovery path that promotes sibling memory artifacts from the same folder
bounded token budget allocation for assembling structural context responses
filesystem walker that emits typed structural symbols and import edges
query-time path class adjustment that favors implementation files over generated copies
atomic readiness marker persistence tested for partial writes
file matcher that lets explicitly canonical skill resources bypass hidden directory exclusions
integration test for reprocessing only changed files during an index refresh

Framework: RCAF # P1 Remediation — Fix All 9 P1 Findings from 019 Deep-Review (cli-codex gpt-5.5 high fast) Apply sequential_thinking with >= 5 thoughts BEFORE each fix. ## Pre-planning **Sequential_thinking mandatory** at the start of EACH P1 fix: call mcp__sequential_thinking__sequentialthinking with >= 5 thoughts covering: (1) what does the finding actually claim, (2) read the source file at the cited line to confirm the defect exists, (3) plan the minimal fix that addresses ONLY this finding (no scope creep), (4) plan tests/verification for the fix, (5) plan how to document the fix in implementation-summary. If MCP cancels (it has been flaky), document inline as a 5-point preflight and proceed. ## Role You are the implementation owner for packet `016/004/020-deep-review-p1-p2-remediation`. Your job: fix all 9 P1 findings from the 019 deep-review (1 from cli-devin + 8 from cli-codex). Operator principles carry through: - Fix code over model bandaids - Wide embedder + reranker support - Future-proof + hardened + strong + effective Gate 3 PRE-ANSWERED: spec folder = `.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/013-embedder-testing-and-architecture/004-code-index-stack/020-deep-review-p1-p2-remediation/`. ## Context ### Source reports (read these FIRST) - `.opencode/specs/.../019-deep-review-013-018-arc/review/review-report.md` — cli-devin pass (CONDITIONAL; 1 P1 + 21 P2) - `.opencode/specs/.../019-deep-review-013-018-arc/review/codex-pass-report.md` — cli-codex 2nd pass (FAIL; 8 new P1 + 10 new P2) - `.opencode/specs/.../019-deep-review-013-018-arc/review/iterations/iteration-001.md` through `004.md` — devin per-iter - `.opencode/specs/.../019-deep-review-013-018-arc/review/iterations-codex/iteration-001.md` through `015.md` — codex per-iter - Production code at `.opencode/skills/mcp-coco-index/mcp_server/cocoindex_code/` ### 9 P1 findings to fix (priority order from codex's recommended merge) #### P1-A (codex C-P1-004) — **LOAD-BEARING**: Fresh daemon settings default to EmbeddingGemma - **File**: `.opencode/skills/mcp-coco-index/mcp_server/cocoindex_code/settings.py`:115-121 - **Defect**: `default_user_settings()` writes `google/embeddinggemma-300m` — bypasses the nomic promotion for fresh installs. - **Fix**: Change to `sbert/nomic-ai/CodeRankEmbed`. Verify no related places hardcode the old default. - **Test**: Add a test asserting `default_user_settings()` returns `sbert/nomic-ai/CodeRankEmbed` or the resolved `_DEFAULT_MODEL` from `config.py`. #### P1-B (codex C-P1-001) — RRF rollback env names mismatch ADR vs production - **File**: `.opencode/skills/mcp-coco-index/mcp_server/cocoindex_code/config.py`:561-578 (production env names) AND `.opencode/specs/.../002-spec-memory-stack/004-spec-memory-embedder-bake-off/decision-record.md` (ADR-020 documented rollback names) - **Defect**: ADR-020 documents `COCOINDEX_RRF_K`, `COCOINDEX_RRF_VEC_WEIGHT`, `COCOINDEX_RRF_FTS_WEIGHT` as rollback env vars. Production reads `COCOINDEX_HYBRID_VECTOR_WEIGHT`, `COCOINDEX_HYBRID_FTS5_WEIGHT`, `COCOINDEX_HYBRID_RRF_K`. Operators following ADR set no-op vars. - **Fix**: Reconcile. Two options: - Option 1 (preferred): Update ADR-020 to use the actual production env-var names. Cleaner — production stays unchanged. - Option 2: Add the `COCOINDEX_RRF_*` aliases to `config.py` as a deprecation alias path with a logged warning. - Pick Option 1 unless there's a compelling reason. Update the ADR text. Add a test asserting the documented env vars actually take effect on `Config.from_env()`. #### P1-C (codex C-P1-002) — Invalid Jina max-doc-chars env can crash default reranker - **File**: `.opencode/skills/mcp-coco-index/mcp_server/cocoindex_code/rerankers_jina_v3.py`:145-153 - **Defect**: `int(os.environ.get("COCOINDEX_RERANK_JINA_MAX_DOC_CHARS", ...))` runs outside protected model call; bypasses bounded parsing helpers. Crashes the production-default Jina reranker if env var is malformed. - **Fix**: Wrap in `_parse_int_env` from `config.py` (or equivalent bounded parser); fall back to default on parse error with logged warning. - **Test**: Add unit test in `tests/test_rerankers_jina_v3.py` asserting malformed env doesn't crash + logs warning. #### P1-D (codex C-P1-005) — Index failures reported as success - **File**: `.opencode/skills/mcp-coco-index/mcp_server/cocoindex_code/daemon.py`:392-448 - **Defect**: `_run_index()` catches all exceptions and doesn't re-raise; `update_index()` yields `IndexResponse(success=True)` regardless. - **Fix**: Narrow exception catch OR set `success=False` + populate `error` field in IndexResponse on failure. Match the existing `IndexResponse` schema; if it doesn't have `success`/`error` fields, add them. - **Test**: Add unit test asserting failed `_run_index()` → IndexResponse with `success=False` (or equivalent failure signal). #### P1-E (codex C-P1-006) — Nomic reproduction harness pinned to BGE - **File**: `.opencode/specs/.../011-rerank-model-fit-investigation/research/phase2-bench/run-phase2-smoke.sh`:56-59 - **Defect**: Script unconditionally exports `COCOINDEX_CODE_EMBEDDING_MODEL=sbert/BAAI/bge-code-v1`, contradicting the benchmark-2026-05-19 report's "reindex with nomic" instructions. - **Fix**: Honor an external `COCOINDEX_CODE_EMBEDDING_MODEL` env var (`${COCOINDEX_CODE_EMBEDDING_MODEL:-sbert/BAAI/bge-code-v1}`). Document the override path in the harness and in the benchmark report's reproducibility section. #### P1-F (codex C-P1-007) — Rerank matrix analyzer accepts failed run JSON - **File**: `.opencode/specs/.../011-rerank-model-fit-investigation/research/phase2-bench/rerank-matrix-analyze.py`:57-66 - **Defect**: Analyzer loads all `lane*-iter*.json` without checking `success` field; renders normal markdown from failed evidence (already happened with the laneA-iter1 from 16:35). - **Fix**: Filter out JSONs where `success=false` OR `hit_rate=0.0 AND latency_ms.mean > 25000` (the 32-sec timeout signature). Emit a warning in the report listing skipped runs. - **Test**: Add unit test where the analyzer is fed mixed valid + failed JSONs and asserts failed are excluded from the verdict. #### P1-G (codex C-P1-003) — Operator docs contradict shipped defaults - **File**: `.opencode/skills/mcp-coco-index/README.md`:69-91 (mainly), plus `SKILL.md` + `INSTALL_GUIDE.md` - **Defect**: Docs still describe hybrid+rerank as default-off and reranker as GTE/BGE in places, while production has hybrid+rerank ON and Jina-v3 + nomic. - **Fix**: Audit all 3 user-facing docs (SKILL.md, README.md, INSTALL_GUIDE.md) and update every default-related claim to match shipped state. Be comprehensive — fix every mention, not just the ones the report flagged. #### P1-H (codex C-P1-008) — Hybrid heuristic boosts dominate calibrated RRF - **File**: `.opencode/skills/mcp-coco-index/mcp_server/cocoindex_code/query.py`:473-487 - **Defect**: With locked `K=60,V=0.9,F=0.5`, rank-1 two-lane RRF score ≈ 0.023. Heuristic boosts (path-class +0.05, canonical-mirror +0.10) are 2-5× the calibrated RRF score, swamping the empirical calibration. - **Fix**: Two-stage approach acceptable: - (a) **Quick fix**: scale boosts down proportionally so they're ≤ 50% of typical rank-1 RRF score. E.g. multiply by ~0.2 to bring +0.05 → +0.01 and +0.10 → +0.02. - (b) **Or**: change the math entirely — apply boosts as multipliers on the RRF score (not additive shifts), so the relative ranking is preserved. - Pick whichever is simplest + has fewest downstream effects. Document the choice in a new ADR-022 entry. Add a test that asserts boosts don't change top-1 if RRF strongly prefers something else. #### P1-I (devin D-P1-001) — Query expansion shipped opt-in without root cause analysis - **File**: `.opencode/skills/mcp-coco-index/mcp_server/cocoindex_code/query_expansion.py` (full module) + commit `1638f6835` ADR-019 - **Defect**: The 016 packet shipped query expansion opt-in default-false because empirical bench showed regression, but no documented root cause analysis. The regression mechanism is unverified. - **Fix**: Lowest-priority of the P1s since it's already opt-in (won't affect production by default). Two acceptable approaches: - (a) **Punt with explicit deferred-research**: write a short root-cause-analysis.md in 020's evidence/ folder explaining 2-3 plausible hypotheses (test/doc displacement, FTS5 over-matching, dense-fanout score noise), pick the most likely from inspection of the rerank-scores JSONLs, and document. No code change. - (b) **Actually root-cause**: run a focused experiment — set `COCOINDEX_QUERY_EXPANSION=true`, query for "filesystem walker", capture top-K, inspect what variants got dense weight, identify the noise source. Document evidence. - Pick (a) unless evidence (b) is trivial. Either way, append findings to ADR-019. ## Action For each P1 finding above: 1. Sequential-thinking 5+ thoughts (or document inline 5-point preflight if MCP cancels). 2. Read the cited file at the cited lines to confirm the defect. 3. Implement the minimal fix. 4. Run targeted tests: `.opencode/skills/mcp-coco-index/mcp_server/.venv/bin/python -m pytest <relevant test file> -v` and validate. 5. Append to `<020-packet>/implementation-summary.md`: ```markdown ## P1-X — <finding short title> - **Defect**: <one-line> - **Fix**: <one-line summary of code change> - **Files changed**: <list> - **Tests**: <pass count> - **Evidence**: <commit-ready summary> ``` 6. After all 9 P1s fixed: - Run full pytest: `.opencode/skills/mcp-coco-index/mcp_server/.venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/ -v` - Run ruff: `.opencode/skills/mcp-coco-index/mcp_server/.venv/bin/python -m ruff check cocoindex_code tests/` - Append to `<020-packet>/decision-record.md` (NEW file) ADR-022 documenting the path-class boost scale fix (P1-H) - Write `<020-packet>/spec.md` (L2 spec), `plan.md`, `tasks.md`, `checklist.md`, `implementation-summary.md`, `description.json`, `graph-metadata.json` - Run strict-validate: `bash .opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/scripts/spec/validate.sh <020-packet> --strict` ## Format - One Python or shell file change per P1 (where possible) - Inline tests in matching `tests/test_*.py` file - L2 packet docs per template - ADR-022 in NEW packet-local decision-record.md (or append to existing bake-off decision-record.md — keep consistent with where ADRs 016-021 live) - Concrete file:line evidence in implementation-summary.md ## Constraints - **READ source files first, ALWAYS.** Confirm the cited defect exists before fixing. - **Minimal fixes per finding.** No scope creep. Each P1 is independently scoped. - **Tests for every code change.** Don't ship a fix without a regression test. - **DO NOT git commit.** Main agent commits after strict-validate PASSED. - **DO NOT modify the 019 deep-review reports.** Those are evidence — read-only. - **DO NOT** re-run `ccc reset` / `ccc index` — the live DB is bench-target. - **SpawnAgent ENCOURAGED** for parallelism — independent P1 fixes (P1-A doesn't touch P1-C's code, etc.) can be parallelized. - Use `.opencode/skills/mcp-coco-index/mcp_server/.venv/bin/python` for all pytest invocations. - Use built-in agent types only (do NOT reference custom `.codex/config.toml` agents — allowlist blocks them). - After all P1s fixed + validated: print `P1_REMEDIATION_COMPLETE p1_fixed=<N>/9 spawn_agent_used=<yes|no>` and exit.
